message:
Complete write_result path parameter across cmd_sync

- Add local_path to remaining write_result calls in cmd_sync
- Ensures consistent path field in all NDJSON result entries
- Improves accuracy of conflict resolution commands

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-606","title":"Implement ensure_dependencies()","description":"**Full dependency check flow**

## What
Orchestrates the complete dependency verification flow.

## Why
One function to verify all deps are ready before main processing.

## Flow
1. Check git (always required)
2. Check gh installed -> prompt install if missing
3. Check gh authenticated -> prompt auth if not
4. Optional: Check for gum and suggest

## Implementation
```bash
ensure_dependencies() {
    # Git is mandatory
    if ! command -v git &>/dev/null; then
        log_error "git is required but not installed."
        return 3  # Dependency error
    fi
    
    # gh is needed for private repos
    if ! check_gh_installed; then
        prompt_install_gh || return 3
    fi
    
    # Check again after potential install
    if ! check_gh_installed; then
        log_error "gh installation failed or was declined."
        return 3
    fi
    
    # Check authentication
    if ! check_gh_authenticated; then
        prompt_gh_auth || return 3
    fi
    
    log_debug "All dependencies satisfied"
    return 0
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- Checks all deps in order
- Prompts when possible
- Fails cleanly when not
- Returns exit code 3 for dep errors","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:31:04.230391434-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-03T16:31:04.230391434-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented in SECTION 8.1 of ru script","labels":["deps"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-606","depends_on_id":"bd-602","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:10.414463431-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-606","depends_on_id":"bd-603","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:10.443175209-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-606","depends_on_id":"bd-604","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:10.472119717-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-606","depends_on_id":"bd-605","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:10.500715497-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"bd-607","title":"Test non-interactive mode dependency flow","description":"**Test deps in non-interactive mode**

## What
Verify dependency checks work correctly without user interaction.

## Why
CI/automation must fail fast with clear errors, never hang waiting for input.

## Test Cases
```bash
# Should fail immediately with instructions
ru sync --non-interactive  # When gh missing
ru sync --non-interactive  # When gh not authed

# Should work with GH_TOKEN
GH_TOKEN=xxx ru sync --non-interactive
```

## Expected Behavior
- Never prompts
- Fails with exit code 3
- Provides actionable error messages
- Works with GH_TOKEN

## Acceptance Criteria
- No hangs in non-interactive mode
- Clear error messages
- GH_TOKEN authentication works","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:31:27.702694717-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-03T16:31:27.702694717-05:00","close_reason":"Non-interactive mode implemented: can_prompt() checks prevent prompts in --non-interactive mode","labels":["deps","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-607","depends_on_id":"bd-606","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:10.530964903-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"bd-608","title":"Make gh CLI optional for public repos","description":"**Fall back to git clone for public repos**

## What
Make gh CLI optional when only syncing public repositories.

## Why
Many users only sync public repos. Requiring gh installation for this is unnecessary friction. The original plan over-relied on gh.

## Design Change
1. Try `git clone https://github.com/owner/repo` first for HTTPS URLs
2. If that fails with auth error, THEN check for gh
3. Only require gh if:
   - User has private repos in their list
   - Public clone fails with auth error
   - User explicitly wants gh features

## Implementation
```bash
do_clone() {
    local url="$1"
    local target_dir="$2"
    
    # Try plain git clone first (works for public repos)
    if output=$(git clone --quiet "$url" "$target_dir" 2>&1); then
        log_success "Cloned: $repo_name"
        return 0
    fi
    
    # If auth error, try gh
    if [[ "$output" =~ (Authentication|403|401) ]] && check_gh_installed && check_gh_authenticated; then
        local clone_target
        clone_target=$(url_to_clone_target "$url")
        if gh repo clone "$clone_target" "$target_dir" -- --quiet 2>&1; then
            log_success "Cloned (via gh): $repo_name"
            return 0
        fi
    fi
    
    log_error "Clone failed: $repo_name"
    return 1
}
```

## ensure_dependencies() Change
Don't fail if gh is missing. Instead:
- Warn that private repos won't work
- Continue for public repos

## Acceptance Criteria
- Public repos clone without gh
- Private repos work with gh
- Clear messaging about what requires gh
- `ru doctor` shows gh status as optional","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:31:50.845755683-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-03T16:31:50.845755683-05:00","close_reason":"Enhancement deferred: current implementation requires gh but can be enhanced later to try plain git clone first for public repos","labels":["deps","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-608","depends_on_id":"bd-6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:13.558501167-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-608","depends_on_id":"bd-602","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:13.590518915-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"bd-7","title":"Phase 7: URL & Path Parsing","description":"**EPIC: Repository URL Parsing & Path Resolution**

## Goal
Implement robust parsing of all GitHub URL formats and layout-aware local path resolution.

## Rationale
Users will throw every URL format at us: HTTPS, SSH, shorthand (owner/repo), with or without .git suffix. We must handle them all correctly. Additionally, the local path depends on the configured layout (flat, owner-repo, full).

## Supported URL Formats
- https://github.com/owner/repo
- https://github.com/owner/repo.git
- git@github.com:owner/repo.git
- github.com/owner/repo
- owner/repo (assumes github.com)

## Layout Strategies
- flat: /data/projects/repo (simple, backwards compatible)
- owner-repo: /data/projects/owner/repo (avoids collisions)
- full: /data/projects/github.com/owner/repo (multi-host ready)

## Collision Detection
With flat layout, owner1/utils and owner2/utils collide. We must detect and warn about this before processing.

## Success Criteria
- All URL formats parse correctly
- Paths resolve correctly for all layout modes
- Collisions are detected and reported","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:24:14.265311545-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-03T16:24:14.265311545-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented all URL parsing functions: parse_repo_url, normalize_url, url_to_local_path, url_to_clone_target. All tests pass.","labels":["parsing","urls"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-7","depends_on_id":"bd-2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:08.160038502-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"bd-701","title":"Implement parse_repo_url()","description":"**Parse all GitHub URL formats**

## What
Function to extract host, owner, and repo from various URL formats.

## Why
Users will provide URLs in many formats. We must handle them all correctly.

## Supported Formats
1. https://github.com/owner/repo
2. https://github.com/owner/repo.git
3. git@github.com:owner/repo.git
4. github.com/owner/repo
5. owner/repo (assumes github.com)

## Implementation
```bash
parse_repo_url() {
    local url="$1"
    local -n _host=$2
    local -n _owner=$3
    local -n _repo=$4

    # Normalize: strip .git suffix
    url="${url%.git}"

    if [[ "$url" =~ ^git@([^:]+):(.+)/(.+)$ ]]; then
        _host="${BASH_REMATCH[1]}"
        _owner="${BASH_REMATCH[2]}"
        _repo="${BASH_REMATCH[3]}"
    elif [[ "$url" =~ ^https?://([^/]+)/([^/]+)/([^/]+)$ ]]; then
        _host="${BASH_REMATCH[1]}"
        _owner="${BASH_REMATCH[2]}"
        _repo="${BASH_REMATCH[3]}"
    elif [[ "$url" =~ ^([^/]+)/([^/]+)/([^/]+)$ ]]; then
        _host="${BASH_REMATCH[1]}"
        _owner="${BASH_REMATCH[2]}"
        _repo="${BASH_REMATCH[3]}"
    elif [[ "$url" =~ ^([^/]+)/([^/]+)$ ]]; then
        _host="github.com"
        _owner="${BASH_REMATCH[1]}"
        _repo="${BASH_REMATCH[2]}"
    else
        return 1
    fi
}
```

## Using nameref
We use bash nameref (-n) to return multiple values. Requires Bash 4.3+.

## Acceptance Criteria
- All formats parse correctly
- Returns 1 for invalid URLs
- Strips .git suffix automatically","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:24:14.288322773-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-03T16:24:14.288322773-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented all URL parsing functions: parse_repo_url, normalize_url, url_to_local_path, url_to_clone_target. All tests pass.","labels":["parsing","urls"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-701","depends_on_id":"bd-7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:10.559767172-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"bd-705","title":"Implement sanitize_path_segment()","description":"**Sanitize path components**

## What
Remove or replace unsafe characters from path segments.

## Why
Malicious or unusual repo names could cause filesystem issues. Defense in depth.

## Implementation
```bash
sanitize_path_segment() {
    local segment="$1"
    # Remove leading/trailing whitespace
    segment="${segment#"${segment%%[![:space:]]*}"}"
    segment="${segment%"${segment##*[![:space:]]}"}"
    # Replace potentially problematic characters
    segment="${segment//\//_}"
    segment="${segment//\\/_}"
    segment="${segment//:/_}"
    echo "$segment"
}
```

## Characters to Handle
- Slashes (/ \)
- Colons (problematic on Windows)
- Leading dots (hidden files)
- Control characters

## Acceptance Criteria
- Dangerous characters replaced
- Empty segments rejected
- Normal names pass through unchanged","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:56:12.946839267-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-03T16:56:12.946839267-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented sanitize_path_segment() with comprehensive character replacement and safety checks","labels":["parsing","safety"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-705","depends_on_id":"bd-7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:10.707274655-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"bd-706","title":"Create scripts/test_parsing.sh","description":"**Unit tests for URL parsing**

## What
Test script to verify URL parsing works for all formats.

## Why
Parsing is critical. Bugs here cause repos to be cloned to wrong locations or fail entirely.

## Test Cases
```bash
# HTTPS URLs
assert_parses "https://github.com/owner/repo" "github.com" "owner" "repo"
assert_parses "https://github.com/owner/repo.git" "github.com" "owner" "repo"

# SSH URLs
assert_parses "git@github.com:owner/repo.git" "github.com" "owner" "repo"

# Shorthand
assert_parses "owner/repo" "github.com" "owner" "repo"

# With host
assert_parses "github.com/owner/repo" "github.com" "owner" "repo"

# Normalization
assert_normalizes "git@github.com:owner/repo.git" "https://github.com/owner/repo"

# Local paths
assert_path "owner/repo" "flat" "/data/projects" "/data/projects/repo"
assert_path "owner/repo" "owner-repo" "/data/projects" "/data/projects/owner/repo"
assert_path "owner/repo" "full" "/data/projects" "/data/projects/github.com/owner/repo"
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- All URL formats tested
- All layout modes tested
- Edge cases covered
- CI can run this script","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:23:24.700261824-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-03T16:23:24.700261824-05:00","close_reason":"URL parsing functions implemented and tested","labels":["parsing","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-706","depends_on_id":"bd-701","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:10.735921071-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-706","depends_on_id":"bd-702","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:10.766284111-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-706","depends_on_id":"bd-703","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:10.796279999-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-706","depends_on_id":"bd-704","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:10.824009347-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"bd-8","title":"Phase 8: Git Operations","description":"**EPIC: Git Clone/Pull Operations with Plumbing**

## Goal
Implement git operations using plumbing commands for reliable status detection, avoiding string parsing of porcelain output.

## Rationale
Parsing 'Already up to date' is fragile - it varies by git version and locale. Git plumbing commands give deterministic, parseable output. We use `git rev-list --left-right --count` for ahead/behind, `git status --porcelain` for dirty detection.

## Key Patterns
- Always use `git -C "$repo_path"` instead of cd
- Capture output with `if output=$(git ... 2>&1); then ... else exit_code=$?; fi`
- Check for divergence by comparing old_head vs new_head after pull
- Support multiple strategies: ff-only (safe default), rebase, merge

## Remote Mismatch Detection
If a local repo exists but origin URL differs from expected, we warn and skip. This prevents accidentally overwriting unrelated repos.

## Success Criteria
- Clone operations work with proper error handling
- Pull operations detect all status conditions (current, updated, diverged, conflict)
- No string parsing of user-facing git messages","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:26:52.457035826-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-03T16:26:52.457035826-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented all git operations: is_git_repo, get_repo_status, get_remote_url, check_remote_mismatch, do_clone, do_pull, do_fetch. All tests pass.","labels":["core","git"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-8","depends_on_id":"bd-7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:08.189443577-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"bd-801","title":"Implement is_git_repo()","description":"**Check if directory is a git repository**

## What
Function to verify a directory is a valid git repository.

## Why
Before pulling, we need to confirm the directory is actually a git repo, not just a regular directory with the same name.

## Implementation
```bash
is_git_repo() {
    local dir="$1"
    [[ -d "$dir/.git" ]] || git -C "$dir" rev-parse --git-dir &>/dev/null
}
```

## Why Both Checks?
- .git directory check is fast
- git rev-parse handles worktrees and bare repos

## Acceptance Criteria
- Returns 0 for valid git repos
- Returns 1 for non-repos
- Works with standard and non-standard git layouts","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:26:52.425291646-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-03T16:26:52.425291646-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented all git operations: is_git_repo, get_repo_status, get_remote_url, check_remote_mismatch, do_clone, do_pull, do_fetch. All tests pass.","labels":["git"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-801","depends_on_id":"bd-8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:10.854102128-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"bd-802","title":"Implement get_repo_status()","description":"**Get repository status using git plumbing**

## What
Function to determine repo status (current, ahead, behind, diverged, dirty) using plumbing commands.

## Why
Parsing 'Already up to date' is fragile (varies by git version and locale). Plumbing commands give deterministic output.

## Implementation
```bash
get_repo_status() {
    local repo_path="$1"
    local do_fetch="${2:-false}"

    if [[ ! -d "$repo_path/.git" ]]; then
        echo "STATUS=not_git AHEAD=0 BEHIND=0 DIRTY=false BRANCH="
        return
    fi

    # Fetch if requested
    if [[ "$do_fetch" == "true" ]]; then
        git -C "$repo_path" fetch --quiet 2>/dev/null || true
    fi

    # Check dirty status
    local dirty="false"
    if [[ -n $(git -C "$repo_path" status --porcelain 2>/dev/null) ]]; then
        dirty="true"
    fi

    # Get current branch
    local branch
    branch=$(git -C "$repo_path" symbolic-ref --short HEAD 2>/dev/null || echo "")

    # Check for upstream
    if ! git -C "$repo_path" rev-parse --verify '@{u}' &>/dev/null; then
        echo "STATUS=no_upstream AHEAD=0 BEHIND=0 DIRTY=$dirty BRANCH=$branch"
        return
    fi

    # Get ahead/behind using plumbing
    local ahead=0 behind=0
    read -r ahead behind < <(git -C "$repo_path" rev-list --left-right --count HEAD...@{u} 2>/dev/null || echo "0 0")

    # Determine status
    local status
    if [[ "$ahead" -eq 0 && "$behind" -eq 0 ]]; then
        status="current"
    elif [[ "$ahead" -eq 0 && "$behind" -gt 0 ]]; then
        status="behind"
    elif [[ "$ahead" -gt 0 && "$behind" -eq 0 ]]; then
        status="ahead"
    else
        status="diverged"
    fi

    echo "STATUS=$status AHEAD=$ahead BEHIND=$behind DIRTY=$dirty BRANCH=$branch"
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- Correct status detection without string parsing
- Works with/without fetch
- Handles missing upstream
- Reports dirty status","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:26:52.449462309-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-03T16:26:52.449462309-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented all git operations: is_git_repo, get_repo_status, get_remote_url, check_remote_mismatch, do_clone, do_pull, do_fetch. All tests pass.","labels":["git"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-802","depends_on_id":"bd-801","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:10.884043543-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
@@ -131,15 +133,22 @@
 {"id":"bd-904","title":"Implement detect_collisions()","description":"**Warn about path collisions**

## What
Function to detect when different repos would resolve to the same local path.

## Why
With flat layout, org1/utils and org2/utils collide. Users need to know before we overwrite.

## Difference from dedupe
- dedupe: Same repo referenced twice (ok, skip second)
- collision: Different repos mapping to same path (warning!)

## Implementation
```bash
detect_collisions() {
    local -A path_to_url
    local collisions=0
    
    while IFS= read -r spec; do
        local url branch local_name
        parse_repo_spec "$spec" url branch local_name
        
        local path
        path=$(url_to_local_path "$url" "$PROJECTS_DIR" "$LAYOUT")
        
        local norm_url
        norm_url=$(normalize_url "$url")
        
        if [[ -n "${path_to_url[$path]:-}" ]]; then
            local existing="${path_to_url[$path]}"
            if [[ "$existing" != "$norm_url" ]]; then
                log_warn "Collision detected: $path"
                log_warn "  URL 1: $existing"
                log_warn "  URL 2: $norm_url"
                ((collisions++))
            fi
        else
            path_to_url[$path]="$norm_url"
        fi
    done
    
    return $collisions
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- Detects different repos at same path
- Doesn't flag same repo twice
- Returns collision count","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:33:03.266022614-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-03T16:33:03.266022614-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented all repo list management functions: parse_repo_spec (namerefs), load_repo_list, dedupe_repos, detect_collisions, get_all_repos. All tests pass.","labels":["lists","safety"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-904","depends_on_id":"bd-703","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:11.391710099-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-904","depends_on_id":"bd-901","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:11.422048533-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"bd-905","title":"Implement get_all_repos()","description":"**Load and merge all repository lists**

## What
Function to load all list files, merge, dedupe, and check for collisions.

## Why
This is the main entry point for getting the list of repos to process.

## Implementation
```bash
get_all_repos() {
    local repos_dir="$RU_CONFIG_DIR/repos.d"
    
    if [[ ! -d "$repos_dir" ]]; then
        log_warn "No repos.d directory. Run 'ru init' first."
        return 0
    fi
    
    # Collect all repos from all .txt files
    local all_repos
    all_repos=$(
        for f in "$repos_dir"/*.txt; do
            [[ -f "$f" ]] || continue
            load_repo_list "$f"
        done
    )
    
    if [[ -z "$all_repos" ]]; then
        log_warn "No repositories configured. Run 'ru add <repo>' first."
        return 0
    fi
    
    # Check for collisions (warning only)
    echo "$all_repos" | detect_collisions || true
    
    # Dedupe and output
    echo "$all_repos" | dedupe_repos
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- Loads from all .txt files in repos.d
- Warns about collisions
- Dedupes results
- Handles empty gracefully","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:33:03.267327372-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-03T16:33:03.267327372-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented all repo list management functions: parse_repo_spec (namerefs), load_repo_list, dedupe_repos, detect_collisions, get_all_repos.  {"id":"bd-905b","title":"Define collision handling policy","description":"**Clear policy for path collision handling**

## What
Define what happens when collision is detected (different repos -> same path).

## Why
Current plan detects collisions but doesn't specify the behavior.

## Policy Options
1. **Warn and skip second repo** (safest, current implicit behavior)
2. **Fail fast** (strict, stops processing)
3. **Prompt user** (interactive only)
4. **Auto-rename with suffix** (risky)

## Chosen Policy: Warn and skip
```
Collision detected:
  Path: /home/user/projects/utils
  Configured: org1/utils (will be synced)
  Skipped:    org2/utils (same path)

To fix: Change layout to 'owner-repo' in config:
  ru config --set LAYOUT=owner-repo
```

## Implementation
- First occurrence wins
- Log warning with resolution hint
- Include in summary as 'skipped:collision'
- Exit code 2 (conflicts) if any collisions

## Acceptance Criteria
- Clear messaging about collision
- First-wins policy
- Resolution hint in output
- Documented in README","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:30:11.250738889-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-03T16:30:11.250738889-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented in ru script SECTION 9B","labels":["lists","safety"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-905b","depends_on_id":"bd-9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:13.858988726-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-905b","depends_on_id":"bd-904","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:13.890572977-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
